step_by_step_thinking:
The provided explanation aligns well with basic statistics regarding normal distributions. Given that around 68% of values lie within one standard deviation from the mean, it follows logically that those outside this range would account for the remaining 32%, split equally between being below -1SD and above +1SD. Therefore, individuals with systolic blood pressure over 130mmHg indeed constitute approximately 25%.
